canonical_value: If the line has a positive sequence impedance of 15 \,\Omega and a zero sequence impedance of 48 \,\Omega, what is the value of \(Z_s\)?
surface_forms: If the line has a positive sequence impedance of 15 \,\Omega and a zero sequence impedance of 48 \,\Omega, what is the value of \(Z_s\)?

canonical_value: If the line has a positive sequence impedance of 15 \,\Omega and a zero sequence impedance of 48 \,\Omega, what is the value of \(Z_m\)?
surface_forms: If the line has a positive sequence impedance of 15 \,\Omega and a zero sequence impedance of 48 \,\Omega, what is the value of \(Z_m\)?

normalized_value.target_text: If the line has a positive sequence impedance of 15 \,\Omega and a zero sequence impedance of 48 \,\Omega, what is the value of \(Z_m\)?

canonical_value:
For a 3-phase transmission line, the voltage drop is given by
$$
\left[\begin{array}{l}
\Delta V_a \\
\Delta V_b \\
\Delta V_c
\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{lll}
Z_s & Z_m & Z_m \\
Z_m & Z_s & Z_m \\
Z_m & Z_m & Z_s
\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c}
I_a \\
I_b \\
I_c
\end{array}\right]
$$
The shunt capacitance of the line can be neglected. If the line has a positive sequence impedance of 15 \,\Omega and a zero sequence impedance of 48 \,\Omega, what is the value of \(Z_s\)?
surface_forms:
For a 3-phase transmission line, the voltage drop is given by
$$
\left[\begin{array}{l}
\Delta V_a \\
\Delta V_b \\
\Delta V_c
\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{lll}
Z_s & Z_m & Z_m \\
Z_m & Z_s & Z_m \\
Z_m & Z_m & Z_s
\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c}
I_a \\
I_b \\
I_c
\end{array}\right]
$$
The shunt capacitance of the line can be neglected. If the line has a positive sequence impedance of 15 \,\Omega and a zero sequence impedance of 48 \,\Omega, what is the value of \(Z_s\)?
